commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ab2c0af205a2a8ededde84f51aa85f9278b6d0cd)
commands.review_summary:
The branch routes Telegram default text sends, bot reply text, and draft previews back through regular `sendMessage` / `editMessageText` HTML/plain fallback paths and lowers Telegram text chunk limits.

PR surface: Source +125, Tests -48. Total +77 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level review: current main still sends normal Telegram text and draft previews through rich-message paths, and the linked issue includes live screenshots showing unsupported client rendering. I did not run a live Telegram repro in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor proof, conflict resolution, and maintainer confirmation of the rich-message rollback shape rather than a safe autonomous repair lane.

target.title: fix(telegram): restore readable default text sends
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #93263 by restoring Telegram's default text delivery, edits, and draft previews to the legacy-readable `sendMessage` / `editMessageText` path instead of the rich raw Bot API path.

This keeps the change scoped to the outage path:
- default text sends now render HTML and fall back to plain text when Telegram rejects formatting
- draft previews now use normal text send/edit calls again
- Telegram default text chunk limits are pulled back to the standard text-message range instead of the rich-message limit
- media delivery, buttons, quote handling, and related transport behavior stay in place

## Why this shape

Issue #93263 and the comment thread point to a Telegram outbound transport regression introduced with the rich-message rollout. The immediate goal here is to restore a readable default for all Telegram-bound agents before revisiting richer transport behavior.

## Verification

Passed:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/draft-stream.test.ts`

In progress:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`
  - currently narrowed to 11 remaining expectation migrations in tests after switching the default text path away from `sendRichMessage`

Not yet run in this draft:
- broader Telegram delivery tests
- live Telegram client proof
- autoreview (intentionally skipped for this draft-first pass per request)
